title: Code Stylometry vs Formatting and Minification - Replication Package
author: BALLA, STEFANO
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Setup 
  Before you proceed with any of the steps, ensure you have Python installed. 
  Step 1: Set up the Python environment. 
 Use the requirements.txt file to install the necessary packages. 
  python -m venv venv   source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows, use: venv\Scripts\activate   pip install -r requirements.txt  
 2. Dataset Creation 
  To create the dataset, navigate to the dataset_creation directory and execute the Jupyter Notebook titled dataset_notebook.ipynb. 
   cd dataset_creation   jupyter notebook dataset_notebook.ipynb  
 Follow the instructions inside the notebook to generate the dataset required for training. 
  3. Training the Neural Network 
  The neural network is trained using the run.py file located in the NeuralNetwork directory. 
 Note: Training can be resource-intensive. In our experiments, we utilized the SLURM job scheduler for this purpose. Due to the high resource demand, we haven't provided a training example within the network_notebook.ipynb. However, you can examine a sample model and understand the training procedure by looking through the provided file.
steps_raw:
Setup 
  Before you proceed with any of the steps, ensure you have Python installed. 
  Step 1: Set up the Python environment. 
 Use the requirements.txt file to install the necessary packages. 
  python -m venv venv   source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows, use: venv\Scripts\activate   pip install -r requirements.txt  
 2. Dataset Creation 
  To create the dataset, navigate to the dataset_creation directory and execute the Jupyter Notebook titled dataset_notebook.ipynb. 
   cd dataset_creation   jupyter notebook dataset_notebook.ipynb  
 Follow the instructions inside the notebook to generate the dataset required for training. 
  3. Training the Neural Network 
  The neural network is trained using the run.py file located in the NeuralNetwork directory. 
 Note: Training can be resource-intensive. In our experiments, we utilized the SLURM job scheduler for this purpose. Due to the high resource demand, we haven't provided a training example within the network_notebook.ipynb. However, you can examine a sample model and understand the training procedure by looking through the provided file.
